days.title: Week 5, Day 1: The Letter त (ta)

words.example_sentence_target: गर्मियों में तरबूज खाना अच्छा लगता है।
words.example_sentence_native: It feels good to eat watermelon in the summer.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Garmiyon mein tarbūz khānā acchā lagtā hai.

days.pronunciation_tip: This is a dental 't'. Touch the tip of your tongue to the back of your upper teeth. It's much softer than the English 't'.
days.cultural_note: India has a wide variety of delicious tropical fruits that are enjoyed during the hot summer months.

days.title: Week 5, Day 2: The Letter थ (tha)

words.example_sentence_native: There is hot tea in the thermos.

days.pronunciation_tip: The 'थ' sound is the aspirated version of 'त'. Make the dental 't' sound while releasing a puff of air. It is NOT the 'th' from 'the'.
days.cultural_note: 'Chai' (tea) is an integral part of daily life in India, and carrying it in a thermos is very common.

days.title: Week 5, Day 3: The Letter द (da)
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'द' sound is a dental 'd'. Touch your tongue to the back of your upper teeth. It's softer than the English 'd'. Not like the retroflex 'ड'.
days.cultural_note: Saying 'kripaya' (please) is polite, but often commands are softened by tone of voice or context rather than a specific word.

days.title: Week 5, Day 4: The Letter ध (dha)

words.example_sentence_target: यह एक धनुष और बाण है।
words.example_sentence_native: This is a bow and arrow.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Yeh ek dhanuṣ aur bāṇ hai.

days.pronunciation_tip: The 'ध' sound is the aspirated version of 'द'. Make the dental 'd' sound while releasing a strong puff of air.
days.cultural_note: The bow and arrow are iconic symbols in Hindu mythology, associated with deities like Rama and Arjuna.

days.title: Week 5, Day 5: The Letter न (na)

words.example_sentence_target: नल से पानी आ रहा है।
words.example_sentence_native: Water is coming from the tap.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Nal se pānī ā rahā hai.

days.pronunciation_tip: The 'न' is a dental 'n'. Your tongue should touch the back of your upper teeth, just like for 'त'.
days.cultural_note: Access to clean tap water is a major focus of development and daily life in many parts of India.

days.title: Week 5, Day 6: The Letter प (pa)
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'प' sound is an unaspirated 'p', like the 'p' in 'spin'. Your lips come together without a puff of air.
days.cultural_note: Kite flying is a very popular activity, especially during festivals like Makar Sankranti and Independence Day.

days.title: Week 5, Day 7: The Letter फ (pha)

words.example_sentence_target: फल सेहत के लिए अच्छे होते हैं।
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Phal sehat ke lie acche hote hain.

days.pronunciation_tip: The 'फ' sound is an aspirated 'p', like the 'ph' in 'uphill'. Note: it can sometimes sound like an 'f', but the true pronunciation is a breathy 'p'.
days.cultural_note: Offering fruits to guests or as part of religious ceremonies ('prasad') is a common custom in India.
